MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Apr-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Amee A. Mikacich of BROWN EASSA & MCLEOD LLP (510-444-3131), specially appearing for defendant / moving party National Cooperative Refinery Association

RULING:
DEFENDANT NATIONAL COOPERATIVE REFINERY ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Specially appearing defendant National Cooperative Refinery Association's motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiffs failed to satisfy their burden of demonstrating that National Cooperative Refinery Association had the requisite contacts with California in order to subject it to personal jurisdiction. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance to conduct jurisdictional discovery is denied. Plaintiffs made no showing that their proposed discovery might (much less, would) produce evidence of any contacts of National Cooperative Refinery Association with California. (See Beckman v. Thompson (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 481, 486-487.). Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson, CSR # 5915 (503/TLJ)
